title: Chapter 37: The God's History
content: The night after the 11th Layer's ultimatum, Roxanne found Silas on the roof of the old lecture hall.
He was staring at the violet sky. His silver hair moved in a wind that wasn't there.
"You're thinking about the war," she said, sitting down beside him.
"I'm thinking about what comes after."
"After what?"
"After the 11th Layer decides I'm too dangerous to leave alive."
"They're after me, not you."
"I am your contract. If they come for you, they come for me."
She looked at him. In the violet light, his red eyes seemed darker than usual.
"Tell me about the war."
"It was not a war. It was a purge."
"What's the difference?"
"A war has two sides. The Primordial War was seven against one."
"You against the Council?"
"Me against the system. The Council was just the instrument."
He was quiet for a moment.
"I was like you once. I saw the Nether-Grid and thought it was broken. I wanted to fix it. I wanted to build something that didn't require suffering."
"What happened?"
"The same thing that's happening to you now. They told me to stop. I refused. They escalated. I escalated back. Eventually, they realized they couldn't beat me in a fair fight."
"So they cheated."
"They sealed me. Not because I was wrong, but because I was winning."
Roxanne looked at her hands.
"They're going to try the same thing with me."
"They will. The only difference is that I was alone. You have me."
"And the card."
"And the card. Which I never had."
She looked at him.
"What was your original form? Before they sealed you?"
Silas was silent for a long moment.
"Do you really want to know?"
"I want to see it."
He looked at her. His red eyes glowed.
"It will hurt to look at."
"I've looked at a lot of things that hurt."
He stood up.
The violet sky flickered.
And Silas let go.
The human form dissolved.

What rose in its place was not a body. It was a wound in reality — a tear where something infinite had pushed through a finite space. Black wings unfolded, not feathered but woven from shadow-threads that drank the light around them. Tentacles of pure darkness extended from a core of absolute nothing, each one tipped with an eye. Hundreds of eyes. Thousands. Blue. Luminous. Unblinking.
They all looked at Roxanne.
She did not flinch.
"You're beautiful," she said.
The eyes blinked. All of them. In sequence.
Then the form spoke — a voice that came from everywhere, from inside her skull, from the air itself.
*You are the first mortal to say that.*
"What did the others say?"
*They screamed.*
She stood up. Walked toward the infinite darkness. Stopped in front of the nearest eye — it was the size of her head.
"I don't scream at things I own."
The eye stared at her.
Then the darkness began to contract. Fold in on itself. The wings retracted. The tentacles withdrew. The eyes closed one by one.
Until Silas stood before her again. Human. Silver-haired. Red-eyed. Formal.
"That was your first look at a god," he said quietly.
"It won't be my last."
"No. The 11th Layer will force you to see worse."
"Then I'll need you at full strength."
"I am at 67 percent now. Your spending has accelerated my recovery."
"What do you need to get to 100?"
"More power than the card can buy directly. I need a catalyst."
"What kind of catalyst?"
"The heart of a dead star. A fragment of the Primordial Void. Or —"
He paused.
"Or one of the seals that bind the 11th Layer's deepest prisoners. Breaking a seal releases tremendous energy. Enough to restore me completely."
"You want me to break into the 11th Layer's prison?"
"I want you to consider the option. Not today. But eventually."
Roxanne looked at the sky. The violet had returned. Steady. Familiar.
"Let me think about it."
"Take your time. The Obligation suspension won't last forever."
"How long do we have?"
"A week. Maybe two. The 11th Layer will find a way around the suspension."
"Then we have a week to get ready."
"Ready for what?"
She turned to him.
"Ready for war."
